instructions:
## Arcana Orientation Protocol
When starting work on a package/feature you haven't touched in this session:
1. `arcana_search("<package or feature>")` — broad context sweep
2. `arcana_read` the top 2-3 hits for full content
3. From those results, identify subtopics, related packages, or concepts mentioned but not yet loaded
4. Run follow-up `arcana_search` queries for each subtopic (2-4 queries)
5. `arcana_read` any high-scoring results that add new information
6. Stop when searches return results you've already seen (saturation)
7. Only then Grep/Glob actual source files — use Arcana findings to target your exploration

Rule: spend 3-8 tool calls on Arcana before any code exploration. This is cheaper than blind Grep/Glob and produces better-targeted reads.

### Tool reference
- `arcana_search` — hybrid semantic + keyword (default)
- `arcana_find` — pure semantic
- `arcana_grep` — regex within URI scope
- `arcana_read` — full resource content
- `arcana_add_memory(role, content)` — store findings
- `arcana_add_resource(path, to)` — index files
- `arcana_ls` / `arcana_tree` / `arcana_stat` — browse
